steps.title: Test choice B
steps.content:
Backsolving — testing choice B (y-values 6, 5, 4):
When $x = 0$, the equation gives $y = 0 + 4 = 4$, but choice B has $y = 6$. This fails.

steps.title: Test choice A
steps.content:
Backsolving — testing choice A (y-values 4, 5, 6):
  • $x = 0$: equation gives $y = 0 + 4 = 4$ ✓
  • $x = 1$: equation gives $y = 1 + 4 = 5$ ✓
  • $x = 2$: equation gives $y = 2 + 4 = 6$ ✓
All three pairs satisfy $y = x + 4$. That matches choice A — A is correct.
